audit_items.proof_trace: The only dynamic allocation in win32.c is in ss_error(): FormatMessage allocates a buffer into msg, which is logged once then freed via LocalFree(msg) inside a NULL guard. After LocalFree(msg) at line 64, the function immediately returns with no further access to msg — no UAF or double-free. All other functions (winsock_init, winsock_cleanup, setnonblocking, strnlen, inet_ntop) use only stack variables with no heap allocation.
